what_it_does: Acceptance test for attaching chat turns to a ui:// address and retrieving them by that address through Suite.attach_chat / Suite.chats_at, verifying round-trip behavior, address isolation, malformed-address rejection, one-source storage in chat.jsonl, unchanged training_signal filtering, persistence across Suite reload, and fail-loud rejection of empty text.
observations: Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 for inbox-hygiene parity before importing Suite/Store.
Creates a temporary FsStore root, discovers nodes, builds a Suite, and deletes the temp directory in a finally block.
Defines two local helpers: check(label, cond) and expect_raises(label, fn).
Tests ui:// addresses including a /@state suffix and rejects non-ui://, code://, bare ui://, and arbitrary malformed strings on both write and read.
Verifies that addressed turns carry address, ts, role, source, and grade fields.
Appends multiple turns at the same address to confirm an append-only thread.
Ensures unaddressed ordinary chat and turns at other addresses do not leak into a requested address thread.
Rejects empty and whitespace-only text with exceptions rather than silent no-ops.
Asserts chat.jsonl exists and that no separate addr_chat.jsonl or chats.jsonl file is created.
Checks that an addressed operator-gold turn appears in training_signal while an addressed twin/assistant turn is filtered out.
Rebuilds a second Suite over the same store root to confirm on-disk persistence of addressed threads.
standouts: The test explicitly frames itself as the inverse of I6: addressed chat must reuse chat.jsonl, not create a parallel store.
It validates training_signal echo-guard behavior remains byte-for-byte unchanged for addressed turns.
It requires fail-loud rejection of malformed addresses on both write and read paths.
It proves persistence by constructing a fresh Suite object over the same on-disk root.
conventions: Follows the script-style acceptance-test pattern with a global PASS counter and small assertion helpers.
Uses COMPANY_TEST_RUN environment toggle consistent with sibling suites.
Uses lambda wrappers to pass callables into expect_raises.
Comments use legalistic section headers and external issue/criterion identifiers (I7, I6, S0, R2, ISSUE-1).
Discovers nodes from a hard-coded NODES path relative to the repository root.
concerns: expect_raises catches any Exception broadly, so it may pass on unintended errors.
The global PASS counter and check side effects are not thread-safe, though acceptable for a standalone script.
Heavy reliance on external context (I6, I7, S0, R2, AGENTS.md) makes the file hard to understand in isolation.
Hard-coded address grammar assumptions in assertions may break if the underlying validator changes.
No pytest/main entry point; it runs as a plain script and exits via assertion failure.
notes: End-to-end acceptance script that locks in address-keyed chat threads as additive fields on ordinary chat records, reusing chat.jsonl and preserving existing training filters.
questions: Where are I6, I7, ISSUE-1, S0, R2, RHM, and AGENTS.md defined?
What is the exact address grammar enforced by Suite.attach_chat and Suite.chats_at?
Why does an assistant-role turn represent a 'twin' source/grade for the echo-guard?
How does COMPANY_TEST_RUN alter Suite or store behavior?
Are Suite.attach_chat and Suite.chats_at already implemented, or is this test driving their implementation?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it is an acceptance test for address-keyed chat attachment and retrieval with the listed criteria.
intention_signals: It tries to establish that addressed chat is not a separate side channel but an ordinary chat record with an extra address field, while keeping malformed-address rejection, downstream filtering, and persistence behavior intact.
novelty: It treats chat-at-address as a first-class member of the existing chat.jsonl stream rather than a parallel store, and explicitly verifies that the training_signal echo-guard still applies to addressed turns.
contribution: A concrete acceptance specification for address-keyed chat attachment and retrieval, covering isolation, malformed-address rejection, one-source persistence, training-filter preservation, and reload behavior, plus reusable local check/expect_raises helpers.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test script for attaching and retrieving chat turns by ui:// address in a Suite, validating round-trip, isolation, malformed-address rejection, one-source chat.jsonl storage, training_signal filter preservation, and persistence across reload.
intention_for_embedding: To prove end-to-end that addressed chat threads ride the existing append_chat path without a parallel store and preserve existing downstream filters.

symbols.does: Asserts a labeled condition and increments a global PASS counter, printing ok or raising a labeled failure.
symbols.description: A small test helper used throughout the script. It takes a human-readable label and a boolean condition, asserts the condition with the label on failure, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints an ok line. It is the primary assertion primitive in this standalone acceptance script.

symbols.does: Asserts that a zero-argument callable raises an exception, incrementing PASS if it does and failing if it does not.
symbols.description: A helper that runs the supplied callable inside a try/except block, increments the global PASS counter when any exception is raised, and raises a failure if the callable returns normally. It catches Exception broadly, so it will treat any raised error as a successful 'fail-loud' outcome.
